title: podman native support for systemd .service file
body:
<!--
---------------------------------------------------
BUG REPORT INFORMATION
---------------------------------------------------
Use the commands below to provide key information from your environment:
You do NOT have to include this information if this is a FEATURE REQUEST

**NOTE** A large number of issues reported against Podman are often found to already be fixed
in more current versions of the project.  Before reporting an issue, please verify the
version you are running with `podman version` and compare it to the latest release
documented on the top of Podman's [README.md](../README.md).  If they differ, please
update your version of Podman to the latest possible and retry your command before creating
an issue.

If you are filing a bug against `podman build`, please instead file a bug
against Buildah (https://github.com/containers/buildah/issues). Podman build
executes Buildah to perform container builds, and as such the Buildah
maintainers are best equipped to handle these bugs.
-->

/kind feature

**Description**

It would be super useful to have podman run a .service file within the container w/o running a full-blown systemd in the container.  Something as simple as specifying `--entrypoint-service=/usr/lib/systemd/system/httpd.service` and auto-selecting the ExecStart line to run with `catatonit` seems like it might work, though a complete replacement might require more work.

**Steps to reproduce the issue:**

1.

2.

3.

**Describe the results you received:**

Not a feature yet.

**Describe the results you expected:**

Can `yum install httpd` then `podman run --entrypoint-service=/usr/lib/systemd/system/httpd.service mycontainer`

**Additional information you deem important (e.g. issue happens only occasionally):**

**Output of `podman version`:**

```
(paste your output here)
```

**Output of `podman info --debug`:**

```
(paste your output here)
```

**Additional environment details (AWS, VirtualBox, physical, etc.):**

comments.body: That would involve podman being able to handle all of the flags of the systemd unit file,  NOT going to happen.  I don't see this as a viable option.
Better might be a systemd feature to start the service then exec catatonit for signal handling, but otherwise get out of the way...
Why would systemd launch another init system?

I was just trying to think of a way to re-use the systemd parsing mechanism but not require the entire systemd stack to be running in the container.  I'd brought it up on IRC and someone said I should open this issue to get feedback on the idea of having podman run a .service file with catatonit.  Sounds like it's beyond the realm of possibility, or not desired.

My hope had been to re-use pre-written .service files to avoid having to write custom entrypoint.sh scripts.
Ping me on IRC and we can discuss  your idea.
did you guys talk? any movement or action required on this?

Probably the most appealing reasons to have this are:
1. to avoid setting the `container_manage_cgroup` seboolean.
2. to avoid multiple processes in the container
3. to avoid writing custom entrypoint.sh for each service

It would be cool to have systemd be able to serialize the config for a single `.service` into something akin to an entrypoint.sh that's run by catatonit for signal handling, but such a feature would likely be relevant to systemd not podman.  I'd like to be able to leverage pre-existing systemd service files without running multiple processes in my containers.  I understand it's a pretty big ask.
@jamescassell Any chance you could open a PR to show your idea?
We really don't see a way to do this easily so I am going to close.
